examTitle: Chapter 7: Work, Energy and Power - Practice Set
source: A Quick Review of E-PCM for Engineering Entrance - Physics
subject: Physics

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If a body is placed on another body which is accelerating by an external force then work done by frictional force on the upper body relative to ground is:
questions.explanation: Friction on the upper body acts in the direction of motion (it is the cause of its acceleration). Since force and displacement are in the same direction, work done is positive (+ve).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A position dependent force F = 7 - 2x + 3x² newton acts on a small body of mass 2 kg and displaces it from x = 0 to x = 5 m. The work done in joule is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body is falling under gravity. When it loses a gravitational potential energy by U, its speed is v. The mass of the body shall be:
questions.explanation: Loss in PE = Gain in KE: U = ½mv² → m = 2U/v².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Under the action of a force, a 2 kg body moves such that its position x as a function of time is given by x = t³/3, where x is in metres and t in seconds. The work done by the force in the first two seconds is:
questions.explanation: v = dx/dt = t². At t=0, v=0; at t=2, v=4 m/s. Work done = ΔKE = ½×2×4² - 0 = 16 J.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A block of mass m is pulled along a horizontal surface by applying a force at an angle θ with the horizontal. If the block travels with uniform velocity, has displacement d, and coefficient of friction is μ, then the work done by the applied force is:
questions.explanation: Normal reaction R = mg - Fsinθ. Friction f = μR = μ(mg - Fsinθ). For uniform velocity: Fcosθ = f → F = μmg/(cosθ + μsinθ). Work = Fdcosθ = μmgdcosθ/(cosθ + μsinθ).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A mass M is lowered with the help of a string by a distance x at a constant acceleration g/2. The work done by the string will be:
questions.explanation: Tension T = Mg - Ma = Mg - Mg/2 = Mg/2. Work done by string = T × x = ½Mgx.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Given that the displacement of the body in metre is a function of time: x = 2t⁴ + 5. The mass of the body is 2 kg. What is the increase in its kinetic energy one second after the start of motion?
questions.explanation: v = dx/dt = 8t³. At t=0, v=0; at t=1, v=8 m/s. ΔKE = ½×2×8² - 0 = 64 J.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The net work done by kinetic friction:
questions.explanation: For a single body, kinetic friction opposes motion so its work is -ve. But for a system (e.g., two bodies sliding against each other), the net work by friction can be negative. However, if one considers relative motion, net can be negative. The question asks net work — it can be negative or involve heat. The answer C (may be negative and positive) accounts for different contexts.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The work done by pseudo forces is:
questions.explanation: Pseudo forces are fictitious forces in non-inertial frames. Their work can be positive, negative, or zero depending on the direction of displacement relative to the pseudo force direction.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The work done by a spring force:
questions.explanation: Spring force W = -½Kx² (when compressing/stretching) or W = +½Kx² (when spring returns to natural length). So it can be both positive and negative depending on the process.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A particle is moving in a conservative force field from point A to point B. U_A and U_B are the potential energies of the particle at point A and B and W_c is the work done by conservative forces in the process of taking the particle from A to B:
questions.explanation: Work done by conservative force = -(change in PE) = -(U_B - U_A) = U_A - U_B.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body of mass m thrown vertically upwards attains a maximum height h. At what height will its kinetic energy be 75% of its initial value?
questions.explanation: At max height h, all KE converted to PE: mgh = ½mv². At height h': KE = 75% of initial = ¾×½mv². By energy conservation: ½mv² - mgh' = ¾×½mv² → mgh' = ¼×½mv² = ¼mgh → h' = h/4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A running man has half the KE that a body of half his mass has. The man speeds up by 1 ms⁻¹ and then has the same KE as that of body. The original speeds of man and body in ms⁻¹ are:
questions.explanation: Let man mass = m, V_m; body mass = m/2, V_b. K_man = ½K_body → ½mV_m² = ½×½(m/2)V_b² → V_m = V_b/2, so V_b = 2V_m. Also (V_m+1)² = 2V_m² → V_m = √2+1. Thus V_b = 2(√2+1).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A uniform chain has a mass M and length L. It is placed on a frictionless table with length l₀ hanging over the edge. The chain begins to slide down. Then the speed V with which the end slides away from the edge is given by:
questions.explanation: Using energy conservation: change in PE = gain in KE. V = √(g/L)(L² - l₀²).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The energy required to accelerate a car from rest to 10 ms⁻¹ is W. The energy required to accelerate the car from 10 ms⁻¹ to 20 ms⁻¹ is:
questions.explanation: W = ½m(10)² = 50m. Energy from 10 to 20 = ½m(400-100) = 150m = 3W.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Power applied to a particle varies with time as P = (3t² - 2t + 1) watt, where t is in second. Find the change in its kinetic energy between time t = 2 s and t = 4 s:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The potential energy between the atoms in a molecule is given by U(x) = a/x¹² - b/x⁶, where a and b are positive constants and x is the distance between the atoms. The atom is in stable equilibrium when:
questions.explanation: F = -dU/dx = -[-12a/x¹³ + 6b/x⁷] = 12a/x¹³ - 6b/x⁷. For equilibrium F=0: 12a/x¹³ = 6b/x⁷ → 2a/x⁶ = b → x⁶ = 2a/b → x = (2a/b)^(1/6).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A car moving with a speed of 40 km/h can be stopped by applying brakes after at least 2 m. If the same car is moving with a speed of 80 km/h, what is the minimum stopping distance?
questions.explanation: Stopping distance S = V²/2a ∝ V². S₂/S₁ = (V₂/V₁)² = (80/40)² = 4. S₂ = 4×2 = 8 m.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: An engine exerts a force F⃗ = (20î - 3ĵ + 5k̂) N and moves with velocity v⃗ = (6î + 20ĵ - 3k̂) m/s. The power of the engine (in watt) is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A child is swinging a swing. Minimum and the maximum heights of swing from earth's surface are 0.75 m and 2 m respectively. The maximum velocity of this swing is (g = 10 m/s²):
questions.explanation: ½mV_max² = mg(h_max - h_min). V_max = √(2g(h_max - h_min)) = √(2×10×(2-0.75)) = √25 = 5 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A stone is thrown at an angle of 45° to the horizontal with kinetic energy K. The kinetic energy at the highest point is:
questions.explanation: At highest point, only horizontal component remains: v = u cos45° = u/√2. KE = ½m(u/√2)² = ½mu²/2 = K/2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: When a long spring is stretched by 2 cm, potential energy is U. If the spring is stretched by 10 cm, the potential energy stored in it will be:
questions.explanation: U = ½Kx². U₂/U₁ = (x₂/x₁)² = (10/2)² = 25. So U₂ = 25U.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A car of mass m is driven with acceleration a along a straight level road against constant external resistance R. When the velocity is v, the power of the engine is:
questions.explanation: Net force = F_engine - R = ma → F_engine = R + ma. Power = F_engine × v = (R + ma)v.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A bread gives a boy of mass 40 kg an energy of 21 kJ. If the efficiency is 28%, then the height can be climbed by him using this energy is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The mass of two substances are 25 g and 89 g respectively. If their kinetic energies are same then their ratio of their momenta is:
questions.explanation: For same KE: P ∝ √m. P₁/P₂ = √(25/89) = 5/√89. √89 ≈ 9.43, which is not a simple ratio. Hence none of these (the ratio 5:√89 does not simplify to any given option).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two springs of spring constant 1500 N/m and 3000 N/m respectively are stretched with the same force. They will have the potential energies in the ratio of:
questions.explanation: U = F²/2K. For same force: U ∝ 1/K. U₁/U₂ = K₂/K₁ = 3000/1500 = 2:1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A simple pendulum of length l has a maximum angular displacement θ. The maximum kinetic energy of the bob of mass m will be:
questions.explanation: Height raised = l - l cosθ = l(1 - cosθ). Max KE = loss in PE = mg × l(1 - cosθ) = mgl(1 - cosθ).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Work done by a simple pendulum in one complete oscillation is:
questions.explanation: In one complete oscillation, the pendulum returns to its starting position. The net displacement is zero and net work done by gravity (conservative) is zero.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body of mass 5 kg is raised vertically to a height of 10 m by a force 170 N. The velocity of the body at this height will be:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A chain of mass M and length l is placed on a table with one sixth of it hanging freely from the table edge. The amount of work done to pull the chain on to the table is:
questions.explanation: Hanging length l₀ = l/6. Work to pull up = Mg l₀²/(2l) = Mg(l/6)²/(2l) = Mgl²/(72l) = Mgl/72.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If the force applied is F and the velocity gained is v, then the power developed is:
questions.explanation: Power P = F × v = Fv.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body of mass 5 kg has momentum of 10 kg m/sec. When a force of 0.2 N is applied on it for 10 sec, the change in its kinetic energy is:
questions.explanation: Initial KE = P²/2m = 100/10 = 10 J. Impulse = 0.2×10 = 2 kg m/s. New P = 10+2 = 12 kg m/s. New KE = 144/10 = 14.4 J. ΔKE = 14.4 - 10 = 4.4 J.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A pump can hoist 9000 kg of coal per hour from a mine of 120 m deep. Then the power in watts, assuming that its efficiency is 75% is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If a man increases his speed by 2 m/s, his kinetic energy is doubled. The original speed of the man is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A ship of mass 2 × 10⁷ kg is at rest. On applying a force of 25 × 10⁵ N, it is displaced through 25 m. The speed of ship after this displacement will be:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A constant force acts on a mass m at rest. The velocity acquired in traversing a specific distance is directly proportional to:
questions.explanation: By work-energy: Fd = ½mv² → v = √(2Fd/m) ∝ 1/√m (for constant F and d).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If the momentum of a certain body be increased by 50%, its kinetic energy will increase by:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A motor drives a body along a straight line with a constant force. The power P delivered by the motor must vary with time t according to:
questions.explanation: Constant force → constant acceleration → v ∝ t. Power P = Fv ∝ t.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: What is the velocity of the bob of a simple pendulum at its mean position, if it is able to rise to a vertical height of 10 cm? (g = 9.8 m/s²)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A force F⃗ = (5î + 3ĵ) newton is applied over a particle which displaces it from its original position to the point r⃗ = (2î - ĵ). The work done on the particle is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The potential energy of a body is given by A - Bx² (where x is the displacement). The magnitude of force acting on the particle is:
questions.explanation: F = -dU/dx = -d(A - Bx²)/dx = 2Bx. So F ∝ x (proportional to x).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A particle is kept at rest at the top of a sphere of diameter 42 m. When disturbed slightly, it slides down. At what height h from the bottom, the particle will leave the sphere?
questions.explanation: Radius R = 21 m. Using energy conservation and normal force = 0 condition: h = 5R/3 = 5×21/3 = 35 m from the bottom.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Calculating the horse power used in pumping 2500 kg of water per minute from a well 12 m deep to the surface. Supposing 40% of the H.P. of the engine during the pumping is wasted. The H.P. of engine will be:
questions.explanation: Output power = mgh/t = 2500×10×12/60 = 5000 W. In HP = 5000/746 = 6.7 HP. Efficiency = 60% (40% wasted). Input HP = 6.7/0.6 = 11.17 ≈ 11.2 H.P.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The work done in pulling up a block of wood weighing 2 kN for a distance of 10 m on a smooth plane inclined at an angle of 15° with the horizontal is:
